channel.title: kubernetes on DataFibers
channel.description: Recent content in kubernetes on DataFibers

item.title: Beyond Basics: Mastering Idempotent Data Pipelines with Apache Airflow and dbt
item.description: Data engineering is the bedrock of any data-driven organization, transforming raw, often chaotic data into structured, reliable insights. While building simple ETL pipelines might seem straightforward, the real challenge emerges when dealing with production-grade requirements: robustness, scalability, observability, and crucially, idempotency. This deep-dive will move past the “Hello World” of data pipelines, exploring how Apache Airflow and dbt can be meticulously combined to construct resilient, idempotent workflows capable of handling failures, re-runs, and evolving business logic without corrupting your data.

item.title: Unraveling Apache Flink's Core: State, Checkpoints, and Event Time Internals
item.description: Unraveling Apache Flink’s Core: State, Checkpoints, and Event Time Internals Apache Flink has solidified its position as a go-to engine for stateful stream processing, capable of handling high-throughput, low-latency, and fault-tolerant computations over unbounded data streams. While many introductory articles touch upon its capabilities, this deep dive aims to peel back the layers, exploring the architectural nuances, core mechanisms, and practical considerations that make Flink exceptionally robust and powerful. We’ll venture beyond the typical overview to dissect state management, fault tolerance through checkpointing, and the intricacies of event time processing.
